mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of declining FEDFUNDS observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.5%, consumer spending collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series signals, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity and stronger corporate output, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: The mechanism reverses direction during expansion, yielding negative 20-day returns in > 50% of rising INDPRO observations, or Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates, or the signal produces negative returns in > 55% of high-GDP quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 220, inflation expectations force monetary tightening and erode real corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL surpasses 220, or the inflation threshold fails to predict return direction with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer confidence and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 20-day returns in > 50% of co-movement instances, or interaction term loses predictive power in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales indicate weakening consumer demand and impending earnings misses, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days during late-cycle downturns.
failure_condition: Retail sales declines fail to predict negative returns, with SPX posting positive 5-day returns in > 55% of falling RSAFS observations, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.
